title: wolftpm-tss: From<TssError> maps TPM-layer errors to Error::Transport — no way to distinguish
description: wolftpm-tss/src/error.rs: From<TssError> for Error maps tpm2_rs_base::errors::TssError (a TPM response code from the TPM's response header) to Error::Transport { code }. This conflates two distinct error classes: (1) transport failures (socket down, device not present) and (2) TPM-layer protocol errors (e.g. TPM_RC_AUTH_FAIL). Callers that want to distinguish 'TPM said no' from 'could not talk to TPM' must inspect the code field. Since we are at 0.1.0, the fix is to add Error::TpmLayer { rc: u32 } before the API fossilizes.
